title: HTTP Semantics, Roles, Representations, and Layered Evidence · Browser and Node Client Syst… · Type-System Trace: Resources Representations
summary: An accessible operations dashboard calls a browser API through service workers and gateways, but one visible error collapses CORS, network, status, and schema failures. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts resources representations increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch protocol evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate a resource from the selected representation and its metadata, content, and current validity. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Distinguish client, server, user agent, origin, intermediary, gateway, proxy, and cache roles per exchange. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
